world.title: Netherlands, Germany to Return Thousands of Artifacts to Ghana
world.desc:
About 2,000 artifacts taken during the colonial era are set to be returned to Ghana from the Netherlands and Germany. The announcement was made at the Next Steps Conference on Reparatory Justice and Restitution in Accra, where Dutch and German ambassadors presented President John Dramani Mahama with a catalogue of the items to return.
Speaking to delegates from more than 80 countries, Mahama cited the Ghanaian-sponsored UN resolution on the impact of slavery, saying its legacy still shapes Africa, the Caribbean, and the wider diaspora today.
During the conference, the German ambassador said the repatriation of the items is evidence of his country's willingness to reckon with its colonial past and deepen ties with Ghana. The Dutch representative described the artifacts as crucial markers of identity for Ghanaian communities rather than simple museum pieces.
